designations.ref: SDN — Islamic State of Iraq and the Levant – Khorasan

leaders.name: Shahab al-Muhajir
leaders.fate: At large; $10M Rewards for Justice bounty

financing.detail: Pseudonymous BTC/USDT donations routed through Telegram fundraising channels
Cross-border hawala flows between Gulf donors and Nangarhar / Peshawar nodes
Local taxation and extortion in eastern Afghan districts under control 2017–2019
Targeted abductions of Hindu and Sikh community members and aid workers

attacks.location: Kabul Hamid Karzai International Airport — Abbey Gate
attacks.summary: Suicide bombing during the US evacuation killed at least 13 US service members and 170 Afghan civilians.
Targeted bombing killed senior Taliban-aligned cleric Sheikh Rahimullah Haqqani.
Suicide bombing of a Hazara prep-class for university entrance exam killed 53, mostly young women.
Attack on Afghan Foreign Ministry during a Chinese delegation visit.
Twin bombings at Qassem Soleimani memorial in Kerman killed at least 95 and wounded 211; ISKP claimed responsibility.

attacks.location: Crocus City Hall, Moscow oblast, Russia
attacks.summary: Armed assault and arson at concert venue killed 145; ISKP claimed; four Tajik suspects detained by Russian authorities.

events.label: ISKP established by Hafiz Saeed Khan and former TTP commanders
US State Dept FTO + OFAC SDGT designations
Sanaullah Ghafari (Shahab al-Muhajir) takes emir role
Kabul Airport Abbey Gate SVBIED — 183 killed

relationships.note: Pledged bay'ah; recognised as Wilayat Khorasan by IS Central.
Founders included disaffected TTP commanders (Hafiz Saeed Khan, Shahidullah Shahid).
Active conflict with Taliban; ISKP rejects Doha agreement and Taliban's nationalist project.

entities.summary: Islamic State Khorasan Province (ISKP) is the Khorasan-region affiliate of the Islamic State, formally constituted on 26 January 2015 by Hafiz Saeed Khan and a circle of disaffected TTP commanders who pledged bay'ah to Abu Bakr al-Baghdadi [src-iskp-1]. The organisation peaked territorially in Nangarhar between 2016 and 2018, was rolled back by joint US-Afghan-Taliban operations, and reconstituted as a cellular insurgency under Sanaullah Ghafari (kunya: Shahab al-Muhajir) from mid-2020 onward [src-iskp-1]. Its operational profile combines high-casualty SVBIED attacks against minority and diplomatic targets in Afghanistan with periodic out-of-area operations — the Abbey Gate bombing during the 2021 US withdrawal [src-iskp-6], the 2024 Kerman bombings in Iran, and the Crocus City Hall assault in Moscow [src-iskp-7]. The Pakistan dimension is dominated by ISKP cells in Mastung (Balochistan), Bajaur, and Peshawar that have repeatedly targeted political rallies and sectarian gatherings. Financing is hybrid: cryptocurrency donations solicited through Telegram, hawala flows from Gulf-based facilitators, and local extortion in areas of historical presence [src-iskp-5]. The group is designated under UN 1267 (QDe.161), US FTO/OFAC, and Pakistan NACTA's proscribed list.

entities.name: Tehrik-e-Taliban Pakistan

leaders.name: Baitullah Mehsud
leaders.fate: Killed in US drone strike, South Waziristan

leaders.name: Hakimullah Mehsud
leaders.fate: Killed in US drone strike, North Waziristan

leaders.name: Maulana Fazlullah
leaders.fate: Killed in US drone strike, Kunar (AFG)

leaders.name: Noor Wali Mehsud
leaders.fate: At large; UN 1267 listed (QDi.427); $3M Rewards for Justice bounty

financing.detail: Local revenue extraction from transport, telecoms, and agricultural produce across KP and ex-FATA
Sustained KFR operations against businessmen, government officials, and political workers in KP
Solicited via Telegram and social media from a transnational Pashtun diaspora
Cross-border value transfer through Pak–Afghan hawala corridors, particularly Khost–North Waziristan

attacks.summary: Six gunmen stormed the Army Public School, killing 149 — including 132 children. Claimed by TTP under Mullah Fazlullah.
Truck bombing killed 60 and wounded 250+. Claimed by TTP-aligned faction.
Friday prayers attack on Shia mosque; ISKP initially claimed but Pakistani investigators attributed to TTP-aligned facilitators.
Inside-job suicide attack on mosque in Peshawar police lines killed 100, mostly police personnel. Claimed by Jamaat-ul-Ahrar splinter aligned with TTP.
Suicide attack on a convoy carrying Chinese engineers working on Dasu hydropower project; 5 Chinese nationals and 1 Pakistani driver killed.

events.label: TTP umbrella formally announced; Baitullah Mehsud declared emir
Baitullah Mehsud killed; Hakimullah Mehsud takes over
Jamaat-ul-Ahrar formally splits from TTP under Omar Khalid Khorasani
Army Public School Peshawar massacre — 149 killed
Fazlullah killed; Noor Wali Mehsud takes emirate
Jamaat-ul-Ahrar and Hizb-ul-Ahrar formally rejoin TTP under Noor Wali
TTP unilaterally withdraws from ceasefire with Pakistan
Peshawar Police Lines mosque bombing — 100 killed

relationships.note: Doctrinal alignment with the Afghan Taliban; Noor Wali Mehsud has publicly pledged bay'ah to successive Afghan emirs.
Operational coordination on the Pak–Afghan border, especially in the Kurram–Khost corridor.
Long-standing safe-haven and ideological cooperation. Several TTP commanders graduated from AQ training cycles in North Waziristan.
Splinter under Omar Khalid Khorasani; rejoined TTP umbrella in 2020.
Some commanders defected to ISKP at founding; ongoing rivalry over jihadi monopoly in KP / Mohmand / Bajaur.
Periodic operational collaboration on anti-Shia targeting in KP and Balochistan.

entities.summary: Tehrik-e-Taliban Pakistan (TTP) was formally constituted on 14 December 2007 as an umbrella for roughly 13 Deobandi militant factions operating in Pakistan's then-FATA and the wider Pak-Afghan tribal belt, with Baitullah Mehsud as its founding emir [src-ttp-1]. The organisation's strategic objective is the establishment of a Sharia state inside Pakistan and the withdrawal of Pakistani security forces from former tribal districts; its operational doctrine combines sustained suicide bombing, complex assaults on military and police targets, and the targeted killing of journalists, political workers, and minority figures [src-ttp-2]. Successive Pakistani military operations — Rah-e-Rast (2009), Rah-e-Nijat (2009–2010), and Zarb-e-Azb (2014–2016) — displaced large numbers of TTP fighters into eastern Afghanistan, where the post-2021 Taliban takeover provided substantially expanded sanctuary. Under Noor Wali Mehsud, who took the emirate after Mullah Fazlullah's 2018 killing, TTP has consolidated several earlier splinters (Jamaat-ul-Ahrar, Hizb-ul-Ahrar) and re-mounted a sustained tempo of attacks across Khyber Pakhtunkhwa from 2022 onward [src-ttp-3]. The 2023 Peshawar Police Lines mosque bombing (100 dead) [src-ttp-6] and the 2024 Bisham attack on Chinese engineers [src-ttp-7] illustrate the group's continued capacity. TTP is designated under UN 1267 (QDe.132), the US FTO list, UK Schedule 2, and the Pakistan NACTA proscribed list.

entities.name: Sanaullah Ghafari

relationships.note: Emir of ISKP under the kunya Shahab al-Muhajir.

entities.summary: Sanaullah Ghafari (kunya: Shahab al-Muhajir) became emir of Islamic State Khorasan Province in mid-2020 and is credited with re-engineering the group as a cellular insurgency capable of high-casualty urban attacks. He is the subject of a $10 million U.S. Rewards for Justice bounty [src-iskp-4].
